proposals.evidence: The authoritative PDF source shows the section heading 'IV' centered between [¶14] and [¶15]. The DB text incorrectly places 'IV' after [¶15]. This is a clear ingestion/structural error where the heading was moved to the wrong location during text extraction.
proposals.note: The PDF is unambiguous: [¶14] appears, then a blank line, then the centered section heading 'IV', then another blank line, then [¶15]. This is standard formatting for opinion structure with section headings appearing before the first paragraph of that section.

coverage_note: Complete opinion (all 16 paragraphs plus section headings verified). Checked: all dates, all numbers, all citations, all case names, all statutory references. Only one structural error found - the misplaced section heading 'IV'. All other content verified as accurate against the PDF source."

Complete textual verification: Opinion 2019 ND 257 (Estate of Blikre) - DB text matches authoritative PDF source exactly when accounting for intentional policy differences (straight ASCII quotes per RULE 9) and known page furniture (page numbers per RULE 8). No substantive transcription errors detected. All citations, party names, statute references, and quoted material verified accurate. OCR scan clean.

proposals.old_exact: and notice against trespass from the circumstances. . . . . 4.

proposals.source_quote: individual authorized by the owner substantially complied with subdivision a and notice against trespass is clear from the circumstances. .... 4. a. An individual, knowing the individual is not licensed or privileged
proposals.evidence: The DB text in paragraph [¶11] omits 'is clear ' from a statutory quote. The PDF source (lines 168-171) clearly shows the statutory language requires 'notice against trespass is clear from the circumstances.' The DB version corrupts this to 'notice against trespass from the circumstances.' Additionally, the phrase 'is clear' appears as orphaned text at the end of the same paragraph, indicating a fragmentation error during ingestion.

proposals.evidence: The DB text has the phrase ' is clear' incorrectly appended after the closing quotation mark of the statutory quote at the end of paragraph [¶11]. This is the orphaned remnant of the missing text from the statutory quote (see related proposal). The PDF source (line 176) ends the quotation cleanly with only the closing quote, no trailing text. This text belongs inside the quotation and was misplaced during transcription.

proposals.old_exact: IN THE SUPREME COURTSTATE OF NORTH DAKOTA
proposals.new_exact: IN THE SUPREME COURT STATE OF NORTH DAKOTA
proposals.source_quote: I N T H E S U P R E M E C O U R T STATE
proposals.evidence: The PDF rendered image clearly shows 'IN THE SUPREME COURT STATE' as two separate words. The pdftotext extraction renders this with inter-letter spacing as 'I N T H E S U P R E M E C O U R T STATE' due to the formatted header layout, but 'COURT' and 'STATE' are distinct words separated by a space in the source. The DB text incorrectly fuses them as 'COURTSTATE'. String verified to appear exactly once in DB text using byte-exact search.
proposals.note: This is a split_join error (fusion) where OCR/ingestion combined two adjacent words. The correction restores the space between 'COURT' and 'STATE'.

coverage_note: Complete opinion scanned. The body text (paragraphs 1-2, all names, citations, and case numbers) verified against source. Only one transcription defect identified: the COURTSTATE word fusion in the header. All other content matches the authoritative PDF source."

Full opinion proofread: caption, substantive opinion text, and signature block. No transcription errors, OCR artifacts, or content defects detected. All citations verified against PDF source. Opinion matches authoritative source PDF except for intentional quote-character style (straight ASCII in DB, curly in PDF) and layout formatting (line wrapping, page breaks).

Full text comparison: DB text verified against PDF source via pdftotext. Normalized whitespace, apostrophe types (straight vs. curly per policy), and layout. All substantive text content matches. Spot-checked: dates (June 23/25, 2018, September 2017, October 25/29, 2018), statute citations (N.D.C.C. § 59-10.1-03(1), § 31-11-02, § 31-11-03, § 1-02-02, § 1-02-08, § 1-02-05), case citations (State v. Bearrunner 2019 ND 29 ¶ 5 921 N.W.2d 894; Horob v. Farm Credit Servs. 2010 ND 6 ¶ 20 777 N.W.2d 611; Choice Fin. Grp. v. Schellpfeffer 2006 ND 87 ¶ 9 712 N.W.2d 855; Desert Partners IV v. Benson 2019 ND 19 ¶ 10 921 N.W.2d 444), key quotes from the statute block, and signatures. All verified identical to authoritative PDF."

Comprehensive comparison of DB text (58 lines, 2099 chars) against authoritative PDF source (73 lines, 2539 chars). All substantive text content verified: opinion paragraphs [¶1], [¶2], [¶3] are identical word-for-word. Layout differences (justices' names on separate lines in PDF vs. single line in DB, line wrapping, centered formatting) are formatting-only and not text errors. Quote style differences (curly in PDF vs. straight in DB) are intentional per policy. No OCR errors, character misreadings, citations errors, dates/numbers errors, or spelling errors detected."

proposals.source_quote:
Dave Houge,                                        Defendant and Appellee
     and
Rod Didier,                                                     Defendant
proposals.evidence: The PDF image (rendered at 300dpi) clearly shows a two-column layout where Dave Houge is designated 'Defendant and Appellee' on the right column opposite his name on the left, while Rod Didier is designated only 'Defendant'. The DB text has collapsed this two-column alignment, resulting in both party names appearing first, followed by both designations in the wrong order, creating ambiguity about which designation applies to which party. The correct order per the source is: Dave Houge [designation], and, Rod Didier [designation].
proposals.note: High-risk party name/caption issue. The DB's current ordering makes the party designations ambiguous. The PDF's column layout clearly shows Houge as 'Defendant and Appellee' and Didier as 'Defendant'.

The DB's linear text rendering has lost this essential alignment information.

coverage_note: Complete text verified: all citations, case names, party names, attorney names, justice names, numerical values (judgment amount $13,106.31, docket No. 20190113, rule citation N.D.R.App.P. 35.1(a)(6)), and substantive content match the authoritative PDF source. The only difference from source PDF is intentional policy-compliant use of straight ASCII apostrophes (U+0027) instead of curly Unicode quotation marks (U+2019), and exclusion of page furniture (Filed line, page numbers, form feeds) which are correctly excluded from the DB."

Full text review completed. Opinion text extracted from both database and PDF source (pdftotext -layout). All substantive content verified character-by-character after normalizing for intentional quote convention (straight ASCII in DB, curly in PDF). Structural differences (page breaks 0x0C, page numbers, headers, caption reformatting, and paragraph line-wrapping) are expected artifact of the ingestion/processing pipeline. No substantive errors detected.

proposals.source_quote:
collided with a
horse-drawn hay trailer. The collision killed one of the five passengers on the hors
proposals.evidence: The DB text contains 'horse- drawn' (incorrectly split with a space before 'drawn'), while the authoritative PDF source shows 'horse-drawn' as a properly hyphenated compound adjective. This is an ingestion/OCR defect where the hyphenated word was incorrectly separated. The fix joins the word back to its proper form, which appears exactly once in the DB and is clearly present in the source.

coverage_note: Complete line-by-line and content verification performed. All 49 paragraphs verified for structural integrity. Key terms, case names, legal citations, and numerical content cross-checked against source PDF. Non-issues investigated include line-wrapped text ('optimal\ntreatment' and 'J.A.H.\nv. Wadle') which are handled by separate deterministic citation-rejoin pass and line-wrapping artifacts, not ingestion errors. The court's own spelling 'aliments' (kidney and liver condition) verified against source. Opinion is substantially clean with only one byte-exact split_join defect identified."

flags.description: Reporter page break markers (*913, *914, *915) embedded in prose text with surrounding blank lines, creating text flow interruption. These markers appear between sentences rather than at proper paragraph/section boundaries. Per rule 8, page furniture is handled by a separate deterministic pass; this notation suggests improper ingestion of page break metadata into prose content.
flags.location_quote:
Here, Holte never


 *913


 informed Scott that he was or would be charged

flags.description: Fragmented citations with stray newlines and commas appearing on separate lines (e.g., '466 N.W.2d 124\n\n , 126 (N.D. 1991)'). Per rule 10, citation-rejoin pass handles this deterministically; documented here as known structural issue.

Markdown formatting contaminant: asterisks around case names and legal references (*Throlson v. Backes*, *See also*, etc.). These are 44 instances of markdown italic notation that should be removed by a separate markup-cleaning pass.
